message:
fix(ffs-dir): track previous entry unconditionally during dir entry removal

The ext4 directory entry removal logic uses record-length merging: when
an entry is deleted, its space is absorbed into the previous entry's
rec_len. Previously, the code only tracked the previous entry offset
when the previous entry had a non-zero inode (i.e., was "live"). This
meant that if two adjacent entries were deleted in sequence, the second
deletion could not find a valid predecessor to merge with, because the
first (now-deleted, inode=0) entry was skipped.

The fix changes the tracking variable from \`prev_live_off\` (only set
for live entries) to \`prev_off_opt\` (set for every entry), matching
the kernel's ext4_delete_entry behavior where any preceding entry --
live or deleted -- can absorb the deleted entry's space through rec_len
extension. This also enables coalescing of adjacent free slots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
